signals.name: Work intake is not prioritized in flow
signals.description: Tickets, guest asks, and exception work enter the operation without a clear priority rule, so the team fights over what matters first.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: intake and demand priority failure expressed through restaurant live flow
evidence_types: tickets worked in the wrong order
urgent work mixed with routine work without triage
team members disagree on what should move first

signals.name: Scope or offer is misaligned to execution reality
signals.description: The live offer asks the team to deliver more complexity, variation, or customization than the current operation can execute reliably.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: scope or offer misalignment expressed through restaurant menu and service complexity
evidence_types: offer complexity slows the core flow
modifier load overwhelms the line or bar
low-frequency items repeatedly disrupt execution

signals.name: Guest expectation surprise appears in service
signals.description: Guests react as though the venue's timing, offer, or service experience is materially different from what they expected coming in.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: customer expectation surprise expressed through restaurant guest reactions
evidence_types: guest says this is not what they expected
surprise about wait, offer, or service conditions
promise and actual experience visibly diverge

signals.name: Opening or reset is incomplete
signals.description: A venue reset starts, but key steps remain half-done, leaving the next service window exposed to avoidable misses.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: opening or reset incomplete expressed through restaurant open-close discipline
evidence_types: reset begins but is not closed cleanly
stations reopen half-restocked or half-checked
handoff into the next period happens with unfinished basics

signals.name: Checker or QC step is absent
signals.description: No one is visibly checking quality, completeness, or readiness before work reaches the guest or the next role.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: quality-check absence expressed through restaurant live execution
evidence_types: no final check before handoff or send
quality escapes because nobody checks
readiness or completeness left to chance

signals.name: Exception repeats without containment
signals.description: A known exception keeps happening again without the venue putting in a temporary containment rule or a stable correction.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: exception repeating without containment expressed through restaurant issue handling
evidence_types: same breakdown repeats within days
known issue returns with no temporary guardrail
recurring exception treated as isolated every time

signals.name: Stock or input information is inaccurate
signals.description: The count, availability, or readiness information the team is using does not match reality closely enough to make clean decisions.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: stock or input inaccuracy expressed through restaurant availability and count control
evidence_types: recorded stock does not match actual stock
team relies on availability information that proves wrong
count-based decisions fail because the inputs are inaccurate

signals.name: Problem is discussed but not closed
signals.description: The venue talks about a recurring problem, but nobody assigns ownership, closes the action, or verifies the fix afterward.
signals.source_ref: universal-signal-gold-set-01: problem discussed but not closed expressed through restaurant follow-through failure
evidence_types: same issue appears in repeated conversations
action named without an owner or closeout
review happens but closure and verification do not

signal_failure_map.source_ref: incoming restaurant work with no clear priority rule is a direct workflow-design failure signal
priority fights also show that role boundaries around triage and ownership are blurred
offer scope that outpaces execution reality is the live operating signature of menu complexity
when scope and flow do not fit, workflow design usually fails alongside offer complexity
guest surprise around timing and conditions often starts with broken promise-setting in the host flow
once the expectation gap is visible, the recovery path has to absorb it or the issue escalates
an incomplete opening or reset is a direct restaurant open-close control failure
half-finished resets also leave the next shift underprepared in practice
when nobody checks the work before send, standards drift becomes inevitable
missing QC is also a practical control-loop failure because the venue is not catching avoidable misses
repeat exceptions with no containment show that the operation is not learning fast enough from known pain
when the same guest-facing issue repeats, the recovery system is also failing to absorb and stabilize it
incorrect stock or readiness information is the clearest direct signal of inventory accuracy failure
bad inputs also point to a wider control-loop gap because decisions are being made off the wrong picture
a problem that keeps getting discussed without closure is a direct signal of a missing learning loop
closure failures also reveal weak managerial ownership of follow-through
